signals.headline: No breaking GDP news confirmed, announced, or resolved right now that would move prediction markets
signals.raw:
No breaking GDP news confirmed, announced, or resolved right now that would move prediction markets.**[1][2][3][4]

The most recent GDP-related update is the US Bureau of Economic Analysis's third estimate from April 9, 2026, downgrading Q4 2025 growth to 0.5% (annualized), slower than prior estimates, amid consumer resilience and energy shocks.[1] No newer announcements appear in the last 12 hour

signals.headline: No Recent Confirmations, Announcements, or Resolutions Noted | NO | LOW confidence**
signals.raw:
No Recent Confirmations, Announcements, or Resolutions Noted | NO | LOW confidence**

Odds update continuously with new info like tweet data releases, but no just-confirmed events (e.g., resolutions or announcements) are reported that would shift markets as of April 22, 2026.[1][2][4] Data is from sources up to April 19, limiting real-time "right now" insights on X trader buzz.[5]
